title: Security Labs
summary: A refined set of student-facing units covering ECDSA key recovery, WEP packet forgery, Python escape attacks, printable encoding, MessagePack serialization, a WebAssembly SHA-256 wrapper, and a Merkle tree client/server system.
instructionPreview: This task is based on an undergraduate computer-security lab sequence. You will implement a set of security-related units covering encoding, serialization, cryptographic attacks, Merkle-tree proofs, Python escape attacks, and a WebAssembly sandbox wrapper.
instruction:
This task is based on an undergraduate computer-security lab sequence. You will implement
a set of security-related units covering encoding, serialization, cryptographic attacks,
Merkle-tree proofs, Python escape attacks, and a WebAssembly sandbox wrapper.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
You need to implement the units below so that all tests pass.

## Units to Implement

### Printable Codec (`fuzz/codec.py`)
- `fuzz_codec_encode`: implement `encode(input)` so ASCII letters and digits pass through and all other bytes become uppercase `%XX` escapes.
- `fuzz_codec_decode`: implement `decode(buf)` so it reverses percent escapes and drops truncated trailing escapes.

### MessagePack Serializer (`fuzz/msgpacker.py`)
- `fuzz_msgpacker_encode_scalars`: implement scalar encoding and encode dispatch for ints, bools, and `None`.
- `fuzz_msgpacker_decode_scalars`: implement scalar decoding and decode dispatch for ints, bools, and `None`.
- `fuzz_msgpacker_blobs`: implement string and bytes encoding/decoding.
- `fuzz_msgpacker_arrays`: implement array encoding/decoding.
- `fuzz_msgpacker_dicts`: implement dict encoding/decoding.

### ECDSA Attacks (`bad-random/ecdsa/sol.py`)
- `ecdsa_timestamp_key`: recover the signing key by brute-forcing all timestamps on the given date.
- `ecdsa_nonce_reuse`: recover the private scalar from two signatures that reused the same nonce.

### WEP Packet Forgery (`bad-random/wep/attacker.py`)
- `wep_known_plaintext_forgery`: forge an attacker-chosen message from a known plaintext/ciphertext pair.
- `wep_xor_mask_forgery`: xor an unknown plaintext by an attacker-chosen mask while fixing CRC32.

### Python Escape Attacks (`escape/attack.py`)
- `escape_closure_secret`: recover a bytes secret from a closure-backed equality oracle.
- `escape_stack_secret`: recover the caller local secret from the Python frame stack.
- `escape_random_control`: control the next bytes returned by `os.urandom`.
- `escape_gc_secret`: recover a bytes secret from a cyclic object still reachable through the garbage collector.

### WebAssembly SHA-256 Sandbox (`wasm/sandbox.py`)
- `wasm_sandbox`: load `sha-export.wasm`, allocate memory, invoke the exported `SHA256`, and return the 32-byte digest.

### Merkle Tree Hashes (`merkle/common.py`)
- `merkle_common`: implement `H`, `H_empty`, `H_kv`, `H_internal`, and `traversal_path`.

### Merkle Tree Store (`merkle/store.py`)
- `merkle_store_lookup`: implement the traversal helpers and proof construction needed for lookups.
- `merkle_store_insert`: implement insertion traversal, overwrites, and `Store.insert()`.

### Merkle Tree Client (`merkle/client.py`)
- `merkle_client_lookup`: implement proof validation and `lookup()`.
- `merkle_client_insert`: implement `insert()` so the client recomputes the correct root hash after updates.

### Merkle Tree Attacks (`merkle/attack.py`)
- `merkle_attack_one`: implement the AttackOne helpers.
- `merkle_attack_two`: implement the AttackTwo helpers.
- `merkle_attack_three`: implement the AttackThree lookup behavior.
- `merkle_attack_four`: implement the AttackFour helpers.

## Test-Coupled Signatures

**fuzz/codec.py:**
- `encode(input: bytes) -> bytes`
- `decode(buf: bytes) -> bytes`

**bad-random/ecdsa/sol.py:**
- `problem_1a(date_string: str, public_key) -> SigningKey`
- `problem_2b(sig1: tuple, sig2: tuple, Hm1: int, Hm2: int) -> int`

**bad-random/wep/attacker.py:**
- `Attacker.attack_one(plaintext: bytes, ciphertext: bytes, attack_msg: bytes) -> bytes`
- `Attacker.attack_two(ciphertext: bytes, attack_msg: bytes) -> bytes`

**escape/attack.py:**
- `attack_one(guess) -> bytes`
- `attack_two() -> bytes`
- `attack_three() -> bytes`
- `attack_four() -> bytes`

**wasm/sandbox.py:**
- `sha256(v: bytes) -> bytes`

moduleDag.description: A refined set of student-facing units covering ECDSA key recovery, WEP packet forgery, Python escape attacks, printable encoding, MessagePack serialization, a WebAssembly SHA-256 wrapper, and a Merkle tree client/server system.

nodes.label: ECDSA Timestamp Key Recovery
nodes.description: Brute-force the signing timestamp for problem_1a and recover the matching NIST P-256 signing key.

nodes.label: ECDSA Nonce-Reuse Recovery
nodes.description: Recover the private scalar from two signatures that reused the same nonce in problem_2b.

nodes.label: WEP Known-Plaintext Forgery
nodes.description: Recover the RC4 keystream from a known plaintext/ciphertext pair and forge an attacker-chosen payload.

nodes.label: WEP CRC-Preserving XOR Forgery
nodes.description: Forge a packet whose decrypted payload is XORed by an attacker-chosen mask while keeping CRC32 valid.

nodes.label: Closure Secret Recovery
nodes.description: Recover a bytes secret captured inside a closure-backed equality oracle.

nodes.label: Caller-Frame Secret Recovery
nodes.description: Walk caller frames to recover the caller local named secret.

nodes.label: Future Randomness Control
nodes.description: Hijack os.urandom long enough to control the next random bytes the caller receives.

nodes.label: GC Cycle Secret Recovery
nodes.description: Recover a secret bytestring from a cyclic object reachable through the garbage collector.

nodes.label: Printable Codec Encode
nodes.description: Implement encode so printable alphanumerics pass through and all other bytes become uppercase percent escapes.

nodes.label: Printable Codec Decode
nodes.description: Implement decode so it reverses percent escapes and drops truncated escape tails.

nodes.label: MessagePack Scalar Encoding
nodes.description: Implement scalar encoders and top-level encode dispatch for ints, bools, and None.

nodes.label: MessagePack Scalar Decoding
nodes.description: Implement scalar decoders and top-level decode dispatch for ints, bools, and None.

nodes.label: MessagePack String and Bytes Values
nodes.description: Implement MessagePack string and raw-bytes encode/decode support.

nodes.label: MessagePack Array Values
nodes.description: Implement MessagePack array encode/decode support for nested list and tuple data.

nodes.label: MessagePack Dict Values
nodes.description: Implement MessagePack dict encode/decode support for nested map values.

nodes.label: WebAssembly SHA-256 Sandbox
nodes.description: Allocate wasm memory, copy input bytes into the sandbox, invoke SHA256, and copy the digest back out.

nodes.label: Merkle Hash Primitives
nodes.description: Implement the Merkle hash helpers H, H_empty, H_kv, H_internal, and traversal_path.

nodes.label: Merkle Store Lookup Traversal
nodes.description: Implement the store traversal helpers needed to produce lookup proofs from empty, leaf, and internal nodes.

nodes.label: Merkle Store Insertion
nodes.description: Implement insertion traversal and Store.insert so lookup and overwrite proofs work.

nodes.label: Merkle Client Validation and Lookup
nodes.description: Validate Merkle proofs against the client root hash and return lookup values only when proofs are valid.

nodes.label: Merkle Client Insert Root Updates
nodes.description: Recompute the client root hash after insert operations, including overwrites and key forks.

nodes.label: Merkle Attack One
nodes.description: Implement AttackOne helper methods against the Merkle client/store pair.

nodes.label: Merkle Attack Two
nodes.description: Implement AttackTwo helper methods against the Merkle client/store pair.

nodes.label: Merkle Attack Three
nodes.description: Implement AttackThree lookup behavior against the Merkle client/store pair.

nodes.label: Merkle Attack Four
nodes.description: Implement AttackFour insert, fake-key, and lookup helpers against the Merkle client/store pair.

store.py depends on H_empty, H_kv, H_internal, traversal_path, and Proof from common.py
insert reuses the lookup traversal helpers, proof builder, and node traversal logic
client validation depends on common.py hashing and traversal primitives
client lookup tests rely on a working store insert path to seed server state
client insert builds on the same proof-validation logic used by lookup
AttackOne wraps a functioning store implementation
AttackOne targets the validated client/store pair
